_meta.name: Material-UI (MUI) Framework Patterns
_meta.description: Reusable interaction patterns for any Material-UI (MUI) application. Okta admin console uses MUI. Captures input resolution, dialog detection, table navigation, and component interaction patterns.

frameworkDetection.note: MUI components use class prefixes like MuiButton-root, MuiTextField-root, MuiTable-root

inputResolution.description: MUI inputs may use <label> with htmlFor, aria-label, or placeholder text. Okta specifically uses textarea for search fields and standard inputs for forms.

search_field.note: Okta uses textarea for search fields on People and Groups pages

text_input.resolution: Find parent .MuiTextField-root, then label text inside

select.note: Standard select or MUI Select component

knownLimitations: Okta buttons often use href='#' for JavaScript-triggered actions — cannot rely on href for navigation detection
Some MUI buttons have no text, only icons — use aria-label or class inspection
Okta uses data-se attributes for test stability but they're inconsistent across pages

buttonPatterns.note: Okta wraps many actions in links with href='#' that trigger JS handlers. Click the link element, not just the button.

dialogDetection.note: Okta uses MUI dialogs for Add Person, Create Token, etc.

tabNavigation.note: Okta API page uses tab-based navigation: Authorization Servers, Tokens, Trusted Origins

topBarPattern.note: Okta has persistent top bar with Contact us, notifications icon, and user profile dropdown

sideNavPattern.note: Okta admin console uses a left sidebar with main sections. Navigation is via standard anchor tags. The sidebar may not appear in the accessibility snapshot as navLinks — check links array instead.
